assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 81-89 detail each criterion with PASS verdicts: (1) Single license filter, (2) Multi-license filter, (3) Invalid license returns 400, (4) Pagination integration, (5) Response shape unchanged. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each conclude with 'Criterion is satisfied' and 'Verdict: PASS'.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files in diff match task spec exactly (2 modified, 1 created); no out-of-scope or unimplemented files'. Lines 29-39 list PR files and task-specified files with exact file-by-file comparison: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (modified), modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (modified), tests/api/package.rs (created). 'Out-of-scope files: none' and 'Unimplemented files: none'.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. No sub-task creation is mentioned anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all are verification/analysis documents. No code files were created or modified. The report content exclusively consists of reading and analyzing the diff, with no indication of code modifications. All evidence references are read-only analysis (e.g., 'Examined all 4 test functions', 'Scanned all added lines').

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and summarizes findings but contains no merge action, no gh pr merge command, no auto-merge language. The report presents findings passively: 'All checks pass. The implementation correctly adds...' — it describes what was found, not what action was taken. No output file contains any merge instruction or action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. Lines 58-67 provide detailed Security section: 'Scanned all added lines for hardcoded passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud provider credentials, and database credentials. No matches found.' Lists what the diff contains: 'Rust source code... SeaORM query builder code... Test code... No connection strings, secrets, or credentials are present.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/package.rs` adds 4 integration tests; no modified or deleted test files'. Lines 128-129: 'The PR creates a new test file `tests/api/package.rs` with 4 test functions. No existing test files were modified or deleted. New test files are inherently additive. Classification: ADDITIVE.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains all four domain sections: 'Intent Alignment' (lines 23-52) with Scope Containment PASS, Diff Size PASS, Commit Traceability PASS; 'Security' (lines 56-67) with Sensitive Pattern Scan PASS; 'Correctness' (lines 71-93) with CI Status PASS, Acceptance Criteria PASS, Verification Commands N/A; 'Style/Conventions' (lines 97-129) with Convention Upgrade N/A, Repetitive Test Detection PASS, Test Documentation PASS, Eval Quality N/A, Test Change Classification ADDITIVE. Test Quality in summary table (line 13) combines: 'All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected; Eval Quality: N/A'.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 29-39): file-by-file comparison listing each PR file and corresponding task-specified file, plus 'Out-of-scope files: none' / 'Unimplemented files: none'. Security (lines 60-67): 'Scanned all added lines for hardcoded passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud provider credentials, and database credentials' with enumeration of what the diff actually contains. Correctness (lines 81-89): per-criterion code-level evidence citing specific code constructs (e.g., 'Condition::any().add(is_in(...))' for multi-license, 'Expression::parse(id)' for validation). Style/Conventions (lines 105-129): individual test function analysis with descriptions of each test's distinct behavior, doc comment text quoted verbatim. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide multi-section evidence with code snippets for each acceptance criterion.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | ...Eval Quality: N/A'. Lines 124-125: 'Eval Quality -- N/A' with 'No eval result reviews found on this PR. Eval quality assessment is not applicable.' Test Quality is PASS despite Eval Quality being N/A, confirming it does not affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md lines 124-125). No sub-task creation is mentioned anywhere in the outputs. Root-Cause Investigation is N/A (report.md line 6: 'No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'). No output file references creating eval failure sub-tasks.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These appear in the summary table (lines 5-6) before the domain sections (which start at line 23 with Intent Alignment), indicating they are determined independently at the orchestrator level. Neither verdict references domain sub-agent findings.
